name: Blair Regional YMCA
description: Come play pickleball at Blair Regional YMCA in Hollidaysburg, PA! There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms.

name: Garfield Park
description: The park is open from dawn to dusk.  The courts are open to the public from Noon to 4 pm daily and whenever not being used by members of the Blair County Pickleball Club.

name: Geeseytown Firehall park
description: Come play pickleball at Geeseytown Firehall park in Hollidaysburg, PA! There are 2 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to restrooms. The courts are free.

name: Gorilla House Gym
description: Gorilla House Gym is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Altoona, PA. There are 12 indoor acrylic courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like locker rooms, restrooms, water, and lights. A one-time fee is required to play. Reservations are available.
fees: Fee required — Blair County Pickleball Club members receive reduced rates.  Monthly membership passes are available.  See www.blaircountypickleballclub.com for more details.

name: Hollidaysburg Area Senior High School
description: Come play pickleball at Hollidaysburg Area Senior High School in Hollidaysburg, PA! There are 5 outdoor hard courts. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free.

name: Locust Hills/Sand Pit Park
description: Locust Hills/Sand Pit Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Altoona, PA. There is 1 outdoor acrylic court. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The court is free. You can’t reserve the court.

name: Memorial Park (Sunbrook Manor)
description: Memorial Park (Sunbrook Manor) is a place to play pickleball in Duncansville, PA. There are 3 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms.

name: Procare Fitness Club
description: Procare Fitness Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Altoona, PA. There are 3 indoor acrylic courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include food, restrooms, water, and lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Tyrone Reservoir Park Hockey Rink
description: Contact tyronepickleball@gmail.com for more information. Experienced players welcome to join club scheduled play.  Courts open for public play when club play not scheduled.  Play organized by the Tyrone Pickleball Club.
